observed_facts: episode_reward_mean is 17.85 with very high std 99.65
r_contact dominates reward ratio (0.537) with mean 1.758, low trigger_rate 0.088, and sum 640
repeated_event_exploitation flag is true; contact event toggled 7 times, max toggle count 9 for low_angle
high_reward_low_progress and shaping_goal_mismatch flags are true
no suspected components listed, but hack_score is 0.75
other dense components (distance, velocity, angle, energy) provide consistent but small negative rewards
likely_true_failures: r_contact event_bonus is triggered multiple times due to bouncing, allowing reward hacking without successful landing
the current reward structure encourages repeated shallow contacts instead of a stable landing
likely_false_positives: component_dominance flag is false (dominant component is r_contact ratio only, but other flags indicate exploitation)
root_cause_hypotheses: r_contact is an event_bonus that fires every time any leg contacts the ground and can be triggered repeatedly by bouncing, providing large sparse reward each time, which incentivizes bouncing behavior to maximise reward instead of finishing the episode with a successful landing

reward_editor.rationale: The r_contact event_bonus is exploited by repeated contacts through bouncing. Converting it to a one‑time event removes the incentive to bounce and aligns reward with a single successful landing. This is a direct fix for the observed reward hack.
reward_editor.expected_effect: The agent will no longer gain large reward for repeated shallow contacts, reducing inflated scores and encouraging a stable landing. The dense shaping components still provide guidance, and the one‑time contact bonus preserves a clear landing incentive. Task‑completion rate should improve.
reward_editor.risk_analysis: Making the bonus one‑time may make the final landing reward sparser, but the existing distance/velocity/angle/energy penalties are dense enough to guide the lander. The overall episode reward may decrease, but that is acceptable as it will better reflect actual landing success. No significant risk of making the problem unsolvable.

recommend_next: Convert event_bonus components to one‑time when repeated exploit is detected
applicability_notes: This fix is applicable when a sparse event reward can be triggered many times per episode without advancing the task goal

diagnosis: The r_contact event_bonus is exploited by bouncing, inflating reward without successful landing. Making it one‑time should fix the hack and align reward with landing.
